name: Whole Wheat Oat Scones Dorrie
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Whole wheat flour, less about 1 1/4 cups
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together the dry ingredients: whole wheat flour, sugar, baking powder, cream of tartar, salt, and raisins or currants.
2. In another bowl, whisk together milk and egg until well combined.
3. Pour the wet mixture into the dry mixture and stir until just combined.
4. Gently fold in butter buds using a spatula or wooden spoon until evenly distributed throughout the batter.
5. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and shape the dough into two smaller rounds (about 1/2 cup each).
6. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown on top.
7. Allow scones to cool slightly before serving.
Further variations:
1. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon over the scones before cutting and baking.
2. Substitute candied fruits for raisins or currants during holiday time.
3. For a maple flavor, replace milk with REAL maple syrup, eliminate sugar, and add dried milk.
4. Add 1/2 cup chopped walnuts to either the original recipe or whole wheat batch.
